summary: The diff implements both REQ-1 (black globe background) and REQ-2 (ensure all stations load including currently playing). REQ-2's station fetching is well implemented via dual-batch parallel fetching in radioBrowserApi.ts. However, the diff for GlobeView.tsx and FullscreenGlobe.tsx is truncated, making it impossible to confirm black background settings or the logic to inject the currently playing station into markers when missing from the fetched list.

missing_requirements.requirement: The Three.js scene background in GlobeView and FullscreenGlobe must be set to black (#000000), and any canvas or container wrapper background must also be black.
missing_requirements.reason: The diffs for GlobeView.tsx and FullscreenGlobe.tsx are truncated and do not show explicit scene.background = black or CSS background-color: #000000 settings anywhere in the visible code.

missing_requirements.requirement: The currently playing station must always appear as a marker on the globe even if the API did not return it in the globe station list.
missing_requirements.reason: The truncated GlobeView.tsx diff does not show any logic to inject the currentStation into the stationPoints array when it is absent from the fetched globe station list. This critical acceptance criterion cannot be verified.

hallucinated_features.description: fetchGlobeStations fetches two parallel batches of 500 stations each (by votes AND by clickcount), potentially returning up to ~1000 unique entries before deduplication.
hallucinated_features.reason: REQ-2 requested fetching up to 500 stations maximum to minimize missing entries. The dual-batch approach retrieves up to 1000 raw entries, exceeding the specified maximum of 500.
